findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Risk
findings.description: The current system has significant accessibility gaps that violate WCAG 2.1 AA standards, which poses a high risk for compliance and user experience. Immediate remediation is required to avoid legal repercussions and to serve users with disabilities effectively.
assumptions: Remediation efforts will be prioritized to address critical accessibility issues.

evidence.excerpt: Screen reader cannot navigate course catalog or enrollment flow.
Video player lacks captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.
Accessibility audit found that 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.
Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.
Need to conduct a full accessibility audit, create the VPAT.

recommendations: Prioritize accessibility fixes in the MVP.

findings.title: Screen Reader Navigation Fix Dependency
findings.description: Fixing the screen reader navigation issues in the course catalog and enrollment flow is dependent on implementing ARIA roles and semantic HTML elements. This is a foundational change that will impact multiple areas of the application.
assumptions: All course catalog components will be updated to use semantic HTML.

evidence.excerpt: Screen reader cannot navigate course catalog or enrollment flow.

recommendations: Implement ARIA roles and semantic HTML in the MVP.

findings.title: Video Player Accessibility Improvements
findings.description: The custom video player must be updated to include captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to ensure compliance with accessibility standards. This is essential for the MVP to cater to all users, especially those with disabilities.
assumptions: The video player can be modified to include accessibility features.

evidence.excerpt: Video player lacks captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.

recommendations: Update the video player in the MVP to include accessibility features.

findings.title: Color Contrast Fixes
findings.description: Addressing the color contrast failures across UI components is crucial for accessibility compliance. This should be included in the MVP to ensure a baseline user experience for all users.
assumptions: Design changes can be implemented quickly to improve contrast.

evidence.excerpt: Accessibility audit found that 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Implement color contrast fixes in the MVP.

findings.title: Quiz and Assessment Accessibility Improvements
findings.description: The quiz and assessment forms need to be made accessible to assistive technologies. This includes using proper form elements and ensuring that error messages are conveyed through text descriptions.
assumptions: Form elements can be updated to be accessible.

evidence.excerpt: Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.

recommendations: Update quiz and assessment forms in the MVP.

findings.title: VPAT Implementation
findings.description: Creating and publishing a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) is essential for compliance, especially for government and education sector prospects. This should be planned for V1 after initial accessibility fixes.
assumptions: Resources will be allocated to conduct the audit and create the VPAT.

evidence.excerpt: Need to conduct a full accessibility audit, create the VPAT.

recommendations: Plan for VPAT implementation in V1.

summary: The analysis reveals significant accessibility gaps in the LearnPath system that pose compliance risks and hinder user experience. Immediate remediation is required in the MVP phase, focusing on critical areas such as screen reader navigation, video player accessibility, and color contrast improvements. Subsequent phases should address quiz accessibility and VPAT implementation.
